LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARY COLLIER AND JOHN GRANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT AND THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, AND OVERRULED AS TO THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS SEEK INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AND MUST FILE A CERTIFICATE PER CIVIL CODE SECTION 1369.560. PLAINTIFFS ARE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO COMPLY WITH CIVIL CODE SECTION 1369.560 AND TO ALLEGE PERSONAL LIABILITY OF THE MS. COLLIER AND MR. GRANT PER CIVIL CODE SECTION 1365.7. PLAINTIFFS HAVE ADEQUATELY PLED BOTH INTENTIONAL AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. CONSPIRACY IS A SUFFICIENT BASIS FOR THE IMPOSITION OF CIVIL LIABILITY. THE CLAIMS ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.

THE REMAINING ITEMS ARE NOT CONCLUSORY. A MOTION TO STRIKE IS NOT THE PROPER MEANS TO ATTACK AN ENTIRE CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: john r. browne iii, esq., law offices of john r. browne, 415.421.6700, for plaintiff joe drummond.
michael k. johnson, esq., lewis brisbois, 415.362.2580, for defendants francesca association, mary ann collier and john grant.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: DEFENDANTs MARY COLLIER, JOHN GRANT's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the entire complaint and the third cause of action, and overruled as to the fourth and fifth causes of action. demurrer sustained without leave as to the 6th cause of action. Plaintiffs seek injunctive relief and must file a certificate per Civil Code section 1369.560. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to comply with Civil Code section 1369.560 and to allege personal liability of the Ms. Collier and Mr. Grant per Civil Code section 1365.7. Plaintiffs have adequately pled both intentional and negligent infliction of emotional distress. Conspiracy is a sufficient basis for the imposition of civil liability. The claims are not uncertain. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
